claim: An agent is fundamentally defined by the model taking autonomy to choose which tools to call, call those tools, handle results, and decide next steps.
actionable: When designing agents, prioritize true autonomy for the model to make tool choices rather than pre-programming the exact sequence of steps; this makes agents adaptable to new scenarios.
source_title: Building the future of agents with Claude

claim: Reducing scaffolding in agent prompts allows the model to benefit from improvements in newer versions, whereas heavily scaffolded workflows cap the intelligence gain.
actionable: Minimize guardrails and predefined paths in agent prompts; trust newer models with more autonomy so you automatically benefit from model improvements without redesigning your pipeline.
source_title: Building the future of agents with Claude

claim: The Claude Code SDK provides a lightweight agentic harness that automates tool calling and can be used for any general-purpose agent, not just coding tasks.
actionable: Use the Claude Code SDK (formerly Claude SDK) as your starting point for any agent project; it provides a battle-tested harness for tool calling without locking you into coding-only tasks.
source_title: Building the future of agents with Claude

claim: Deep research tasks like web search and fetch can be accomplished with minimal prompting by giving the model access to server-side tools and letting it iterate autonomously.
actionable: For research tasks, provide the model with web search and web fetch tools, then let it autonomously decide when to search, fetch, and synthesize results without micromanaging the steps.
source_title: Building the future of agents with Claude

claim: Context management becomes critical in long agentic loops; removing older tool call results helps the model focus, but must be done carefully to preserve recent context.
actionable: In multi-turn agent loops, implement context cleanup to remove old tool results, but preserve the most recent context and tombstone removed results so the model understands what's gone.
source_title: Building the future of agents with Claude

claim: Agent memory tools allow models to take notes during execution and review them when stuck, making them more effective at complex multi-step tasks over time.
actionable: Provide agents with a memory tool to record learnings during execution (e.g., 'this API endpoint is fast', 'Wikipedia is better than this site'), then let them review notes when deciding next steps.
source_title: Building the future of agents with Claude

claim: Business value definition is critical when scoping agent projects; articulating expected outcomes like engineering hours saved or manual work reduced helps define scope and prevent overreach.
actionable: Before building an agent, define the specific business value it should deliver (hours saved, error reduction, etc.); this prevents building over-engineered solutions and helps scope the agent correctly.
source_title: Building the future of agents with Claude

claim: Observability becomes essential for long-running agents; without visibility into what the agent did, debugging and tuning become nearly impossible.
actionable: When deploying long-running agents, implement logging and observability from day one so you can debug failures, trace decisions, and tune prompts based on real execution data.
source_title: Building the future of agents with Claude

claim: Computer use capabilities (file system access, Linux tools, code execution) are emerging as the next frontier for giving models true autonomy to solve problems without human intervention.
actionable: Anticipate that future agents will have persistent desktop access and tool setup capabilities; design agent interfaces to support this level of autonomy rather than blocking it.
source_title: Building the future of agents with Claude
